item.title: Ibanez SRMS806-BTT Bass Workshop Multi-Scale Soundgear 2018 - 2022 - Brown Topaz Burst — $1,075
item.description:
$1,075 · Excellent · Super Duper 's Gear

Ibanez SRMS806 – 6-String Multi-Scale Bass – Near Mint Condition – w/ Ibanez Hardcase (Fair Condition)

Super Duper Gear – Curating High-End, Vintage & Collectible Instruments
Houston, TX | Worldwide Shipping | Se habla Español

The Ibanez SRMS806 is a professional 6-string multi-scale bass from the highly respected Soundgear series, designed for modern players who need extended range, ergonomic comfort, and tight, articulate tone across all strings.

Featuring Ibanez’s multi-scale design, the SRMS806 provides improved string tension, clarity, and balance from the low B to the high C. Its slim SR body, fast neck profile, and lightweight feel make it extremely comfortable for extended playing sessions, whether on stage, in the studio, or in technical performance settings.

This bass is in Near Mint Condition and includes an Ibanez hardcase in fair condition.

Specs & Features

  • Model: Ibanez SRMS806
  • Strings: 6-String
  • Construction: Multi-Scale / Fanned Frets
  • Body: Okoume
  • Top: Poplar Burl
  • Neck: 5-Piece Jatoba/Walnut
  • Fingerboard: Panga Panga
  • Frets: 24 Medium
  • Scale Length: 35.5”–33.6”
  • Pickups: Bartolini BH2
  • Electronics: Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-Band EQ
  • Controls: Volume, Balance, Bass, Mid, Treble
  • Switch: 3-Way Mid Frequency Switch
  • Bridge: MR5S Mono-Rail Bridge
  • Hardware: Black
  • Case: Ibanez Hardcase Included (Fair Condition)

Condition

  • Bass: Near Mint Condition
  • Minimal signs of use
  • Electronics fully functional
  • Neck is straight and stable
  • Frets show excellent life remaining
  • No cracks, breaks, or structural repairs
  • Case: Fair condition with cosmetic wear

Why This SRMS806?

  • Professional 6-string multi-scale design
  • Excellent string tension and note clarity
  • Bartolini BH2 pickups
  • Lightweight and ergonomic Soundgear body
  • Near mint bass with included hardcase
  • Great for gospel, fusion, metal, worship, and studio work

Important Notice
The included Ibanez hardcase is in fair condition and shows cosmetic wear. Please review all photos carefully.

What You See Is What You Get
The bass and case shown in the photos are the exact items you will receive. No stock images are used.

Ask Before You Order
Feel free to contact us with any questions regarding condition, specs, or setup.

Follow Super Duper Gear for boutique, vintage, and collectible instruments shipped worldwide from Houston, Texas.

https://reverb.com/shop/super-duper-s-boutique

item.title: Sadowsky MetroLine 24-Fret Vintage J/J bass-chambered alder body 5 String Dark Lake Placid Blue — $3,200
item.description:
$3,200 · Excellent · PWS

Rare opportunity to own a beautiful Sadowsky 5-string 24-fret Metroline (German made) Vintage J/J bass with chambered alder body finished in dark lake placid blue, morado fingerboard, and legendary Sadowsky electronics. I bought this new, directly from Roger in NYC, in 2022. He set it up for me and it plays, sounds, and looks just like it did on that day. Last three pics are from when it was new (for reference). Selling only because of chronic health issues. Please ask any questions. Will ship in the Gator case, properly packaged and insured to continental US only. Thank you.


Sadowsky 5-24 MetroLine Vintage

MODEL 5-24 MetroLine Vintage (made in Germany)

SERIAL #1536-22

FINISH Dark Lake Placid Blue Metallic

BODY Chambered Alder with quick release control plate

WEIGHT 8.54 lbs

NECK Maple with satin finish, 12″ radius, 1 7/8″ nut width

FINGERBOARD Morado with satin finish

PICKGUARD Parchment

HARDWARE Chrome

PICKUPS Sadowsky Humcancelling J/J 

PREAMP Sadowsky preamp with vintage tone control 

CASE Gator GL-BASS lightweight rigid

item.title: S Martyn Blaze 28” 6-string Bass. Comfy, Lightning-Fast Player. Tonal Beast! — $3,100
item.description:
$3,100 · Excellent · Bryan's Cool Music Gear

S Martyn Blaze 28” 6-string Bass.  Comfy, Lightning-Fast Player.  Tonal Beast!

This bass may be small, but it packs a mighty punch.  If you’ve been playing short/medium scale basses (30”-32”) and you’ve never tried one of Samuel Martyn’s 28” basses, you owe it to yourself to check one out. IMO, they play faster and sound every bit as good as my longer scale basses (and I have owned all the major brands… Fodera, Alembic, MTD, Ritter, Jerzy Drozd, etc.).  You’ll experience clean, crisp mids/highs, as well as thick, punchy bass tones. 

  • Blaze 28”
  • 6 Strings
  • Body wood: Pinus Elliotti
  • Neck wood: Cumaru
  • Fingerboard: Roasted Pau Ferro
  • Inlay: Dots
  • Bridge: Nova Guitar Parts
  • Finish: Matte
  • 15mm spacing
  • 7.4 lbs
  • June 2025 Build
  • Includes nice S Martyn case

I’m moving strictly to 5-string basses these days, so I’m selling this 6-string.  It’s currently strung E to F, but can readily accommodate a B to C setup with a fat, punchy, low-B tone.

If you want to see how incredible this bass sounds and plays, check out S Martyn’s premiere artist, Robson Albuquerque, on Instagram.  This bass was built to his specifications.

Free shipping in CONUS

Let me know if you have any questions.

Bryan

item.title: AIO Wolf S11-7 7-String Bass Guitar - Wine Red 006 — $749
item.description:
$749 · Brand New · All In One Guitar [AIO Custom & Wolf]

You will be receiving the exact guitar pictured.

We will install a New alkaline Duracell battery and will Setup with a New Set of D'addario XL Nickel Wound Strings ( 18-32-45-65-100-130 guage ).

To make sure you get the best-tailored setup, below are the setup options you can choose from, and also, please let us know your level of playing/playing style and your age(Just curious for our records :))

1. Medium Action 4/32 inch on treble side5/32 on bass side
2. Medium Low Action 3/32 -4/32
3.Low Action (more low on bass side) 2.5/32- 3 /32

Also, Please choose the neck relief option as well.

A. Traditional Style(more like Fender type)
B. More relief so the low to high is very even.

**Please note, for setup #3, weather and temperature changes during shipping could affect our setup(buzzing issue). And if you play it with heavy stroke with #3 setup then there will be a buzz on the fingerboard. Generally, when you get your instrument it might be slightly changed(Intonation, neck relief, and fret level) during the delivery with the carrier, especially the tuning.To To make sure you get the best-tailored setup, below are the setup options you can choose from, and also, please let us know your level of playing/playing style and your age(Just curious for our records

This stunning S11 7-string bass is one of our absolute favorites. Not only does it look incredible, but its playability and tone are superb. Expect warm, tight low-end, silky highs, and an even sound throughout the neck. Each bass undergoes a professional setup before it leaves our shop, ensuring it feels comfortable in your hands. The ebony fingerboard adds a growl and punch that's hard to beat. Buy with confidence!

Specifications

  • Body: Constructed from Solid African Mahogany, providing a deep, rich tone with excellent sustain. Topped and backed with Solid Flame Maple, this bass not only sounds incredible but also looks stunning in its finish.
  • Neck: Solid Maple Neck-Through design reinforced with Solid Walnut (4 pieces Maple and 3 pieces Walnut). This configuration offers superb stability and sustain, making it a joy to play.
  • Fingerboard: Sleek Ebony, delivering a smooth and responsive playing experience.
  • Headstock: Topped with Solid Flame Maple, adding a touch of elegance and visual appeal. 
  • Top and Back: Solid Flame Maple
  • Position Marks: Side dots to keep your playing on point. 
  • Tuners: Grover tuners for reliable tuning stability, ensuring your instrument stays perfectly in tune.
  • Truss Rod: Dual action, allowing for precise neck adjustments to suit your playing style. 
  • Scale Length: 35''
  • Battery: Powered by dual 9V batteries, providing consistent and reliable performance.
  • Neck thickness at !st Fret:  22.9 mm
  • Neck thickness at 12th Fret: 24.33 mm 
  • Bridge width: 3 7/8'' 
  • Bridge Spacing: 15 mm 
  • Nut Width: 60.9 mm
  • Width at the 12th Fret: 86mm
  •  Pick Up: AIO Custom Single Space Mini Humbucker (Coiled by Accurate CNC machine) 
  • Lower Bout: 11''
  • Upper Bout: 13.5" 
  • Total Length of Bass: 46.5"  

Controls

2-Position Mini Toggle Switch: Selector for on and off the volume

Master Volume push/pull for active or passive

Center Tapped Front and Rear Pickup Mix Volume

Center Tapped Bass (only works at an Active position)

Center Tapped Middle (only works at an Active position)

Center Tapped Treble (only works at an Active position)


About All in One Guitars

We run a humble one-stop guitar shop in Los Angeles. We are a team of great guitar/bass players, builders and repairers. The owner of AIO and all of the staff and luthiers are professional musicians with 30-40 years of experience. You can find many guitars/basses anywhere but we are confident that the ones from our shop are better than the ones you get from big corporate shops. Anyone that has done business with All in One Guitars can confirm that we focus on playability so our setup standard is incredibly high. Our guitar set-ups are custom shop quality and the playability and feel is off the charts. You are paying not just for the price of the instrument, but also for our close attention and care for it. We deal with honesty and integrity and we make sure that the instrument feels good in your hands. We want to share our quality instruments and let everyone enjoy and love music with the ease of playing.

We hope that you can make purchases with the confidence of knowing that not a single instrument leaves our store without receiving our utmost care.

item.title: Floor Model Fender Geddy Lee Jazz Bass - Slight Damage — $699
item.description:
$699 · Good · Jay’s Bass Shop

Geddy Lee Jazz Bass - Floor Model

This bass has some paint damage in TWO places, however the bass plays great and is a gem! This was a demo unit in a store in Colorado, but is now being sold to someone to have that great Jazz Bass feel! Please refer to the photos for visuals and message me for more information.

The Fender Geddy Lee Jazz Bass is a modern-day warrior built to the specifications of the Rush frontman himself. Closely modeled on Geddy's own trusty '72 edition, this J Bass sports a cool vintage vibe with plenty of old-school J Bass tone to match. With high-end hardware, electronics, and renowned Fender quality, you don't have to be a Rush fan to recognize that this is one of the coolest J Basses around.

Specs Worthy of a Prog-Rock LegendWhile Geddy Lee may be most famous for his unmistakable falsetto, he's demonstrated no lack of bass virtuosity in his 40 plus years with Rush. Geddy's signature Fender features an ultra-playable neck with a thin C-shape profile preferred by many rock bassists. The pickups are dual vintage Jazz Bass single-coils which capture the rich low-end and spunky mid-range punch of a '70s J Bass. This bass also includes a Leo Quan Badass II bridge for great articulation and sustain.

Vintage Fender VibeThe Geddy Lee signature rocks a classic Fender look just like Geddy's own '72. The neck sports stylish black rectangle inlays, as well as distinct black binding on the maple fretboard. These black touches are finely complemented by the chrome hardware and vintage-style headstock.

Features:- Modeled after Geddy's own J Bass
- Classic Fender style with chrome hardware, black block inlays and neck binding
- Ultra smooth C-shaped neck
- Sturdy Leo Quan Badass II bridge

- BODY MATERIAL: Alder
- BODY FINISH: Polyester
- BODY SHAPE: Jazz Bass

- BRIDGE PICKUP: American Vintage Single-Coil Jazz Bass
- NECK PICKUP: American Vintage Single-Coil Jazz Bass
- CONTROLS: Volume 1. (Middle Pickup), Volume 2. (Bridge Pickup), Master Tone
- SWITCHING: None
- CONFIGURATION: SS

- BRIDGE: Geddy Lee High Mass Bridge

- CONTROL KNOBS: Black Plastic
- NECK PLATE: 4-Bolt Vintage-Style

- OTHER FEATURES: Black Fingerboard Binding with Black Rectangular Shaped Position Markers, Slim Neck Profile,

- NECK MATERIAL: Maple
- NECK FINISH: Urethane
- NECK SHAPE: Thin "C"

- FINGERBOARD MATERIAL: Maple

item.title: Vox Phantom IV Bass 1969 Black — $3,420
item.description:
$3,420 · Very Good · Wesley's Shop

Here is a vintage VOX Phantom IV bass. Made in Italy around 1969 this bass comes with original teardrop hard case. The pickguard has shrunken a bit and is cracking (like most are). The finish has a cool stripped cracked patina showing its vintage state.  The Phantom IV Basses had the "trapezoidal" body which is so in demand today. Many are just hanging in some studio display. Therefore they are extremely hard to find in vintage condition.  It is unique for its distinctive,  shape, which became part of the iconic representation of the . Originally made in , manufacturing was later relocated to .    used a Phantom IV bass. The Vox Phantom IV bass is indelibly connected to Phil "Fang" Volk of Paul Revere and the Raiders. Phil Volk strengthened his tie to the Vox Phantom IV by taping his nickname, "Fang," to the back side of his bass. Phil often flipped the Phantom IV over to expose his nickname to the audience.  of  played a Phantom IV bass. Robbie Scharf of the Cowsills played a phantom IV Bass. Michael Jay of the Fuzztones played a Phantom bass. The Phantom IV bass featured two single coil pickups with four pole pieces. A rotary switch was used for pickup selection. A single volume and tone control were also included. The 1966 US Vox catalog described the Phantom IV Bass as follows: "Rugged and powerful solid body in striking Phantom design; fast neck with ebony fingerboard; two husky, 4-pole bass pick-ups; adjustable bridge for low action control; fast action pick-up selector switch. By 1965 the majority were built in Recanti, Italy. The Phantom IV bass is one of Vox's most imposing instruments; unlike the short-scale, very thin-necked layout of most of the company's basses these are built with a full 34" scale on a big wide neck closely based on the Fender Precision. The two 4-pole pickups provide a wide range of tonal options; there are not a lot of 1960's basses that provide this combination of practical playability and wicked cool looks! This is a superb example,, and comes complete with the original Vox oblong hard case. The Phantom IV is garage rock classic. Overall length is 49 in. (124.5 cm.), 12 3/8 in. (31.4 cm.) width, and 1 5/8 in. (4.1 cm.) in depth, measured at side of rim. Scale length is 34 in. (864 mm.). Width of nut is 1 3/4 in. (44 mm.). The original finish on the body has some longitudinal checks which are very cool and classic vintage indeed. . There are a few small chipped areas of finish on the body edges, the most noticeable on the top edge behind the bridge, and a couple on the headstock. (see photos). There are no alterations or repairs. There is minor fret or play wear. This one also has a very nice straight and solid neck. This striking black Phantom is a fine player! It's a crowd pleaser of a bass. You'll have tons of interested inquires about this rare axe. And, it sounds great! This is NOT a Japanese knockoff. It's the real deal... untouched.

Tough as nails and thick as elephant skin, the Fender® Troy Sanders Precision Bass® is the next step forward in gnarly, grinding bass tone and high-voltage versatility.

Colossal power is delivered by Tim Shaw-designed custom pickups, custom-voiced 18V preamp, and a Fender HiMass™ vintage bridge. Other key features include an alder body in a mesmerizing Silverburst finish with matching headstock and '70s style Fender logo , distinctive chromed control plate, maple neck with custom '70s P Bass® "C" shape, and 9.5"-radius slab rosewood fingerboard with pearloid block inlays and 20 medium jumbo frets.

The final touches are Sanders' own signature decal on the back of the headstock, Mastodon "Remission" logo on the neck plate, and custom "Emperor of Sand" inlay at the 12th fret. With razor-sharp clarity and titanic muscle, The Fender® Troy Sanders Precision Bass® brings the crushing power and signature sonic mayhem of Mastodon right to your fingertips.

item.title: Dunable Gnarwhal DE Bass Silverburst — $950
item.description:
$950 · Excellent · Keep Portland Geared

2023 Dunable Gnarwahl DE bass in gloss silverburst. Features a 35” scale, dual Dunable p-style pickups, a mahogany body and neck, and a bound ebony fretboard. I have owned both USA and DE Dunable basses and these are incredible values and stand up toe-to-toe with their US-made counterparts. Plus, no wait time from the US custom shop. The DE Gnarwhal is currently not in production, so grab this one while you can!

This bass is in excellent condition both cosmetically and functionally. The dual p-bass configuration absolutely cuts through a full band mix. I am surprised you do not see it more often because it is so versatile and useful. With both pickups full on, you get a super p-bass sound that is unique while still holding a precision bass fundamental tone. The neck pickup soloed is like an overwound, more aggressive p-bass, and the bridge soloed is thick and fully-bodied - thicker than a jazz bass bridge soloed.

There are no finish flaws or issues: no dings, cracks, chips, breaks, and no electronic issues - everything works as it should and the bass has been well cared for. Had a recent pro setup with a fresh set of strings and is set up with low action. It is tuned and intonated to E-standard. As with any stringed instrument, expect some shifts and adjustments during summer shipping. You may want to bring it to your tech to have it setup to your preference.

Will include a non-original gig bag for extra protection while shipping.

item.title: Jawbreaker - Antigua Fender P-Bass '78 Chris Bauermeister — $6,000
item.description:
$6,000 · Very Good · Chris Bauermeister's One Time Jawbreaker Bass Gear Sale

This sale is for a 1978 Fender Precision Bass in Antigua finish, not only as modified and used on stage by me, Chris Bauermeister from Jawbreaker, but also including all of the original components to set it back up in its vintage 1978 configuration.


I bought this as my 1st backup for the initial Jawbreaker reunion at the 2017 Riot Fest (at the time I only had my original from the 90s) and I used it on tour through 2022, sometimes as my main bass (after I acquired a 3rd and retired my original). In time I have acquired too many antiguas (I now have 3 besides this one), so I'm letting this go.


As with all my basses, I modified this from the original by substituting a Leo Quan Badass bridge and putting in EMG active pickups (the ones in there now are actually a set I used in the '90s) - since 2018 I have been leaving off the tone knob because I always turn it to treble / direct (this also means I don't have to rout out extra space for a battery, though this bass has the routing). Since the scratch plate on every 1970s Antigua I have owned has cracked at the output jack (I think the finish does something to the plastic?), I also replaced the original with the black one here. A final note: the top machine head is a little "pranged" from when I purposefully dropped it during sound check in Austin in 2018 because no one was paying attention when I was trying to say I couldn't hear anything - I circled what LOOKS like a dent in it, but that's just the reflection of the other machine head (see side photo). The next to last photo shows me playing this guitar in Austin in 2018 - before I changed the scratch plate or removed the tone knob.


But wait, there's more! I also kept all the original components that I removed, so this bass can be restored to the same vintage condition I bought it in. Of course the neck and the body are the original matching 1978 components. Included is also the original Antigua scratch plate (broken at output jack, missing that piece) which shows the same initial 3 numbers on its serial number as the neck: "S86" (though the rest of the digits have worn off with time). Also included are several of the original scratch plate screws, the "ashtray" cover to the bridge (I never had the pick guard), the original bridge with screws, the original Fender pickups (with backing plate and mounting screws) still attached to complete electronics (I think 1 solder point needs to be reattached), knobs and washers for the pots & jack, strap buttons & screws (there are strap lock buttons on now). In addition I can also include a complete afyrr market soldered analog saddle for the EMGs (NOT missing the tone pot) so you can set it up have the full dynamic tone control if you want (last photo)


Please feel free to contact me for any further questions about the bass (or other photos).

item.description:
$299.99 · Excellent · rockstargear4u

From rockstargear4u, this Ibanez AEB5E-BK Acoustic-Electric Bass is in excellent condition!

The Ibanez AEB5E-BK is a versatile acoustic-electric bass that delivers warm unplugged tone along with dependable plugged-in performance. Featuring a single-cutaway body, rosewood fingerboard, built-in Ibanez AEQ2T preamp with onboard tuner, and comfortable neck profile, it's equally at home practicing on the couch, leading worship, recording, or performing live.

This bass has been thoroughly tested and is working exactly as it should. The electronics are quiet, the onboard tuner and preamp function perfectly, the controls operate smoothly, the neck is straight with comfortable action, and the frets show minimal wear.

item.description:
$1,899.95 · Mint · Top 40 Guitars

At Top 40 Guitars

with your purchase, please return it for a refund.

Up for Sale is...


Fender American Vintage II 1960 Precision Bass

MINT with Case!


With a fresh setup in the Top 40 Guitars Custom Shop!

Brand new strings and ready to ROCK!


8.8 Pounds


The Fender® American Vintage II series presents a remarkably accurate take on the revolutionary designs that altered the course of musical history. Built with period-accurate bodies, necks and hardware, premium finishes and meticulously voiced, year-specific pickups, each instrument captures the essence of authentic Fender craftsmanship and tone.


The late '50s to the early '60s was a watershed period in the development of the Precision Bass®, a time of transition that would see the introduction of many new appointments that would become synonymous with the P-Bass®. By 1960, the design of the P-bass had eclipsed the earlier cues culled from the Telecaster® - now the P-bass had a Strat®-style headstock shape, comfort-contours, pickguard mounted electronics and 4-saddle bridge. Adorned in 3-Color Sunburst and topped with a 4-ply tortoiseshell pickguard, the 1960 Precision Bass represented a more refined, enduring aesthetic for the model.


Featuring a premium alder body, Pure Vintage '60 Split-Coil P-Bass pickup and a substantial "C" shape hard rock maple neck mated with a 7.25" radius slab rosewood fingerboard, the American Vintage II 1960 Precision Bass reproduces the quintessential tone and feel of the original.


The instruments in the American Vintage II series are direct descendants of the original Fenders: designed for players with a fine appreciation for vintage Fender tone and feel and built with unmatched quality, down to the last screw. These are Fender electrics in their purest form: Fender American Vintage II, the stuff of legends.


Due to CITES Treaty for Endangered Wood (Mahogany, Rosewood) We no longer ship out of the USA.


Cosmetics:

Mint.

Top 40 Guitars is a Veteran Owned Business!


Thanks For Supporting Veterans!

item.description:
$1,300 · Good · ERIK STOKLEY

Here we have a beautiful ibanez btb7 Le from 2013. This was ibanez first run of 7 string basses, gloss finish with gold hardware. They shortly after switched to different woods and matte finish/cosmo hardware, so this was a limited edition run with only a short number produced.

Sounds beautiful and plays great. Looks like the nut has been cracked and repaired at the b string. Has a few scuffs/dings/swirling etc through the body and neck. Interesting finish seperation around the knot between the pickups. Comes with an ibanez hard case.

item.title: Fender Player II Precision Bass 4-String Bass Guitar Aquatone Finish — $699.95
item.description:
$699.95 · Excellent · Morrell Music Co.

This 2026 Fender Player II Precision Bass is used with "used" stamped on the back of the headstock. It is in excellent condition and shows no dings, dents, scratches, or any obvious playwear. The previous owner changed the pickguard to a good-quality black 3-ply in favor of the original Parchment that would have come on this model. This bass has been professionally set up and plays great! The neck is straight, the action is low and comfortable, and the frets are in excellent condition. Awesome deal on a fantastic playing and sounding Fender Player II Precision Bass in a gorgeous Aquatone Blue Finish!


Step into the spotlight and experience iconic Fender® sound and style with the Player II Precision Bass® — a stage-ready bass guitar with contemporary updates to power your performance and inspire your playing.

The Player II Precision Bass radiates timeless Fender charm, but under the hood, it's primed for today's players. Everything about the neck is designed for fast and fluid playability, from the Modern “C”-profile with silky satin urethane finish on the back to the comfy 9.5”-radius slab rosewood or maple fingerboard with smooth rolled edges and 20 medium jumbo frets. A classic alder body is available in both timeless Fender finishes and never-before-seen colors unearthed from the archives. Player Series Alnico V Split-Coil P bass® pickups offer sweet highs, musical mids and seismic lows that elevate any genre. An adjustable 4-saddle bridge, single-groove steel saddles and open-gear tuners ensure precise tuning stability for the flexibility to explore endless sonic possibilities.

Perfect for crafting your own sound, the Player II Precision Bass has the looks, tone and feel that only a Fender delivers.
